sections.heading: Syntax
Options
sections.body:
`phrase` uses the following terms to construct a query:

| Field    | Type                       | Description                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      | Necessity |
| -------- | -------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------- |
| query    | string or array of strings | String or strings to search for.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 | yes       |
| path     | string or array of strings | Indexed field or fields to search. You can also specify a wildcard path to search.IMPORTANT: To use the phrase operator, you must index the query field as type string with the indexOptions property set to positions or the default value, offsets. To learn more, see Configure String Field Properties.                                                                                                                                                                                                      | yes       |
| slop     | integer                    | Allowable distance between words in the query phrase. Lower value allows less positional distance between the words and greater value allows more reorganization of the words and more distance between the words to satisfy the query. The default is 0, meaning that words must be exactly in the same position as the query in order to be considered a match. Exact matches are scored higher.                                                                                                               | no        |
| score    | object                     | Score to assign to matching search results. You can modify the default score using the following options:boost: multiply the result score by the given number.constant: replace the result score with the given number.function: replace the result score with the given expression.When querying array values, MongoDB Search assigns higher scores if more values in the array match the query.For information on the options for modifying the default score, see Score the Documents in the Results.         | no        |
| synonyms | string                     | Required for running queries using synonyms.Name of the synonym mapping definition in the index definition. Value can't be an empty string.The amount of time that MongoDB Search takes to execute queries that use synonym mappings depends on the number and size of documents in the synonym source collection.For example, a query that uses a synonym mapping that is based on very few synonym documents might be faster than a query that uses a synonym mapping that is based on many synonym documents. | Optional  |

sections.heading: Scoring Behavior
sections.body:
By default, the text operator uses the `bm25` similarity
algorithm to score documents depending on their relevance to the query.

You can change which similarity algorithm is used by specifying a
different similarity algorithm in the `similarity.type` property for
the string fields in your MongoDB Search index definition. To learn how to
configure a MongoDB Search index for the string type, see
How to Index String Fields.

To learn more about the supported similarity algorithms, see
[Score Details](fts-similarity-algorithms).

sections.heading: Examples
sections.body:
The examples in this page use the `movies` collection in the
`sample_mflix` database. After loading the [sample data sets](available-sample-datasets) into your cluster, create the MongoDB Search
index with dynamic mappings and run the example queries on your
cluster. To try the [Synonyms Examples](text-ref-synonyms-eg) examples,
you must also add the [Synonym Source Collection Example](sample-synonyms-coll) to the `sample_mflix` database and then
[Index Definition Examples](synonyms-index-definition-ex) with the
`synonyms` mapping collection.

sections.heading: Single Phrase Example
sections.body:
The following MongoDB Search example performs a basic search of the
`title` field for the query string `new york`. There is no
`slop` in the query and so the `slop` value defaults to
`0`, which means the position of the words must exactly match
the query string to be included in the results. The query also
includes a:

- [$limit](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/limit) stage to limit the output to 10 results.

- [$project](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/project) stage to exclude all fields except
  `title` and add a field named `score`.

sections.heading: Basic Example
Metadata Example
Multiple Phrases Example
